what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet that styles the Streams UI, a token-driven guided workflow runner and launcher. It defines layout, color, typography, spacing, states, and responsive behavior for stream cards, a step progress rail, a runner stage and navigation, step-specific bodies (drop, options, decision, produce, review, gather, candidate actions), launcher buttons, an embeddable StreamHost overlay, a fullscreen runner variant, a SHAPE-phase synthesis layout with a sticky side panel, and mobile-native adaptations.
observations: Uses CSS custom properties extensively for spacing, color, typography, radii, shadows, and timing, with only a few hardcoded pixel values.
Follows BEM-like naming with block .strm, elements __, and modifiers --.
Defines a responsive card grid using auto-fill minmax(280px, 1fr).
The runner progress rail is a horizontal flex list with node states: default, .done, and .on.
Step bodies include distinct patterns: upload drop zone, option chips, decision question, produce chip, review candidate list, gather list, and candidate approve/reject actions.
Candidate actions use hover states tied to status success/error tokens and modifier classes for approved/rejected states.
StreamHost supports both compact inline launchers and a fixed full-screen overlay runner panel.
Fullscreen runner variant centers content at max-width 1080px and uses a sticky bottom navigation bar.
SHAPE phase uses a two-column grid with a sticky side panel that collapses to a single column below 860px.
Mobile adaptations at 640px hide rail labels except the active node, make the overlay fill the viewport, and make the nav sticky at the bottom.
standouts: A single stylesheet builds a complete visual vocabulary for a multi-step workflow runner.
Explicit mobile-native ergonomics: thumb-reachable sticky nav, full-viewport overlay, hidden rail labels.
StreamHost pattern with compact and overlay/fullscreen launcher variants.
SHAPE-phase synthesis layout with a sticky 'what Vi has learned' side panel.
Candidate review micro-interactions with approve/reject hover states and state modifiers.
conventions: BEM-style class naming with blocks, elements, and modifiers.
Token-driven styling via CSS custom properties.
Use of place-items: center for icon tiles.
Use of min-width: 0 to prevent flex/grid overflow.
Desktop-first responsive design using max-width media queries.
Sticky positioning for navigation bars and side panels.
Section comments organizing the file by component area.
concerns: Some class names are heavily abbreviated, which may reduce readability.
A few magic pixel values remain alongside the token system.
Candidate action buttons show only hover states; no visible focus or active styles.
Overlay z-index is hardcoded to 120 rather than using a token.
No visible dark-mode or reduced-motion considerations.
Compact StreamHost overrides many child properties, increasing specificity surface.
A comment references a shared CandidateCard in identity.css, but that cross-file contract is not defined here.
notes: This file is the styling contract for a guided, multi-step Streams workflow runner, covering card galleries, step rails, step bodies, launchers, overlays, fullscreen mode, and mobile-native responsive behavior.
questions: Which CSS custom properties used here are defined elsewhere, and what fallback behavior is expected if they are missing?
Is the candidate card styling in .strm-review--cards fully inherited from identity.css, or are local overrides intended?
Are the step body classes mutually exclusive per stage, or can they be combined within one stage?
How is the width of the understanding progress bar set: inline style, class thresholds, or JavaScript?
What content populates the .strm__synth-side deductions panel and how is it kept in sync with the main stage?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles a token-driven guided workflow runner and launcher for desktop and mobile.
apparent_themes: Reusability and composition through shared components
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to provide a complete, self-contained visual system for an interactive workflow runner that feels like a native app on mobile and a polished card-based tool on desktop, while remaining aligned with an existing design-token family.
novelty: The combination of compact inline launchers, a modal overlay runner, a fullscreen runner variant, and a SHAPE-phase synthesis side panel in one stylesheet is distinctive, along with the explicit understanding progress bar and candidate approve/reject micro-interactions.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, token-driven CSS vocabulary for a multi-step workflow UI, including responsive layouts, stateful step rail, step body patterns, launcher and host variants, and candidate-review interactions that a fused system could reuse as a styling contract.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet defining the layout, tokens, states, and responsive behavior for the Streams guided workflow runner, launcher, StreamHost overlay, fullscreen runner, and SHAPE-phase synthesis panel.
intention_for_embedding: To style a token-driven, desktop-and-mobile-native guided workflow runner and launcher within the Counterpart design system.
